rationale: The citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending reference-price effects from consumer packaged-goods settings to B2B negotiated transactions, and the cited 2001 paper is directly in that CPG internal reference-price/gain-loss literature. Both notes share core constructs (internal reference price, gain vs. loss asymmetry), with the cited paper’s key finding (stronger loss than gain effects) plausibly serving as a foundational comparison point rather than an incidental mention.
